subLocations.name: Inside Sin
subLocations.prose: Inside Sin is a labyrinth of waterfalls and platforms — the mini-map only shows what's immediately nearby. Fiends here are extremely powerful, especially Behemoth Kings (Meteor for ~6,000 to the party), so save often and lean on aeons. Critically, Behemoth Kings use Meteor as a death rattle when killed — avoid triggering that final blow while party HP is low, and don't use White Magic to finish them off. The teleport-pad sections also contain Energy Dragons, which are weak to Bio — Lulu's Bio spell or Poisontouch weapons do solid work here. Chests throughout contain strong endgame equipment including Wakka's Four-on-One and Yuna's Laevatein. The final Save Sphere before the climb to the Tower of Death is your last chance to return to the airship for side quests.

bosses.strategy: HP: 20,000. Two components: the Sinspawn body and a Sin cell attached to it. Attack the body exclusively — the cell will revive the body once if you kill the body first without first destroying the cell, so kill the cell after the body goes down. Genais uses Sin's Venom (moderate party damage + Poison) and Demi — keep HP up and cure Poison promptly. Aeons and multi-target Overdrives make short work of both components.

HP: 80,000. Four Mortiphasms (1 HP each) orbit Seymour and grant him elemental absorption — but they reform at the start of every one of Seymour's turns. Destroy all four before casting any spell each round, every round, or your magic will heal him. Non-elemental attacks (Tidus, Auron, Wakka physicals; Rikku's throws) are always safe. Seymour's Multi-Spell fires off a random number of hits targeting random characters — keep everyone above 2,000 HP and run Protect + Shell. Use Dispel when he casts Protect or Shell on himself. Save Overdrives for bursts between Mortiphasms clears.

The two Yu Pagodas continuously attempt to heal Jecht — don't waste time trying to destroy them. Instead, use Auron's Zombie Attack on Jecht at the start so the Pagodas' cure spells damage him instead of healing him. Reapply Zombie Attack regularly as the status wears off. During Tidus's turns, use the Trigger Command to talk to Jecht; this drains the Overdrive gauge significantly — save it until the gauge is nearly full, and don't bother more than a couple of times. If the Overdrive can't be talked down, summon an aeon to absorb the hit. After Jecht loses 60,000 HP he transforms into his second form — use Rikku's Mighty G mix or similar to stack Haste, Protect, and Shell in one turn, and apply Auto-Life to key characters if available. Throw Mega-Potions at Zombie-afflicted Jecht and empty Overdrives; this is the last real fight of the game.
Unkillable conventionally — Yu Yevon has permanent Auto-Life. The game ends the fight automatically after Braska's Final Aeon. Watch the ending.
